title: CLAUDE.md
text: Three main S3 classes, print/plot/predict/summary/subset methods: patterns — output patterns(). Contains eigenvalues, EOFs (spatial loadings), amplitudes (time series). Created patterns_core.R, class defined patterns_class.R. coupled_patterns — output couple(). CCA results pairing predictor predictand patterns. Defined couple.R. common_patterns — output common_patterns(). Joint EOF across multiple datasets per-source amplitudes. Defined common_patterns.R.

title: Cross-Validation and Tuning
text: EOF CCA analyses require choosing many modes retain (k). modes discard useful signal; many introduce noise. tidyeof provides two cross-validation functions data-driven mode selection: tune_eof() – optimize EOF truncation field reconstruction tune_cca() – jointly optimize predictor EOFs, response EOFs, CCA modes downscaling use temporal cross-validation: time series split folds, patterns estimated training folds, reconstruction prediction skill evaluated held-folds.

title: Cross-Validation and Tuning
code: fine <- system.file("testdata/prism_test.RDS", package = "tidyeof") |>   readRDS()  coarse <- fine |>   st_warp(cellsize = 0.2, method = "average", use_gdal = TRUE, no_data_value = -99999) |>   setNames(names(fine)) |>   st_set_dimensions("band",     values = st_get_dimension_values(fine, "time"),     names = "time")

title: Cross-Validation and Tuning
text: tune_eof() evaluates reconstruction skill across range k values. fold, extracts patterns training data, projects test data onto patterns, reconstructs test field, compares held-observations. Three metrics computed default: RMSE – root mean square error (lower better) cor_spatial – mean spatial correlation per time step (higher better) cor_temporal – mean temporal correlation per grid cell (higher better) Summarize across folds find optimal k:
code: eof_results <- tune_eof(fine, k = 1:8, kfolds = 3) ℹ Computing patterns for 3 folds ✔ Computing patterns for 3 folds [547ms]  Evaluating 8 k values across 3 folds eof_results # A tibble: 24 × 5        k  fold  rmse cor_spatial cor_temporal    <int> <int> <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl>  1     1     1 0.807       0.790        0.998  2     1     2 0.657       0.930        0.998  3     1     3 0.671       0.922        0.997  4     2     1 0.389       0.931        0.999  5     2     2 0.308       0.971        0.999  6     2     3 0.308       0.974        1.000  7     3     1 0.353       0.940        1.000  8     3     2 0.290       0.976        1.000  9     3     3 0.292       0.976        1.000 10     4     1 0.336       0.944        1.000 # ℹ 14 more rows eof_summary <- summarize_eof_cv(eof_results, metric = "rmse") eof_summary # A tibble: 8 × 8       k rmse_mean rmse_sd cor_spatial_mean cor_spatial_sd cor_temporal_mean   <int>     <dbl>   <dbl>            <dbl>          <dbl>             <dbl> 1     8     0.241  0.0443            0.975         0.0206             1.000 2     7     0.250  0.0413            0.974         0.0205             1.000 3     6     0.258  0.0410            0.973         0.0198             1.000 4     5     0.266  0.0410            0.972         0.0196             1.000 5     4     0.284  0.0456            0.969         0.0210             1.000 6     3     0.311  0.0359            0.964         0.0207             1.000 7     2     0.335  0.0466            0.959         0.0242             0.999 8     1     0.711  0.0828            0.881         0.0786             0.998 # ℹ 2 more variables: cor_temporal_sd <dbl>, n_folds <int> eof_results |>   group_by(k) |>   summarize(     rmse_mean = mean(rmse),     rmse_se = sd(rmse) / sqrt(n()),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   ggplot(aes(k, rmse_mean)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = rmse_mean - rmse_se, ymax = rmse_mean + rmse_se), alpha = 0.2) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   labs(x = "Number of EOFs (k)", y = "RMSE", title = "EOF reconstruction skill") +   theme_bw()

title: Cross-Validation and Tuning
text: tune_cca() jointly searches three dimensions: k_pred – number predictor EOFs k_resp – number response EOFs k_cca – number CCA modes (optional; defaults min(k_pred, k_resp)) First, prepare cross-validation folds using prep_cv_folds(). pre-computes EOF patterns maximum requested k fold, grid search truncation fast (just subsets pre-computed patterns). Summarize find best parameter combination:  Using fewer CCA modes min(k_pred, k_resp) acts additional regularization. search k_cca explicitly:
code: cv_folds <- prep_cv_folds(   coarse, fine,   kfolds = 3,   max_k_pred = 5,   max_k_resp = 5,   weight = TRUE ) ℹ Computing patterns for 3 folds ✔ Computing patterns for 3 folds [536ms]  cv_folds  ── Cross-Validation Folds ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Folds: 3 Common time steps: 36 Max predictor EOFs: 5 Max response EOFs: 5  ── Pattern Options ──  Scale (predictor): FALSE Scale (response): FALSE Rotate: FALSE Monthly: FALSE Weight: TRUE cca_results <- tune_cca(   cv_folds,   k_pred = 1:5,   k_resp = 1:5 ) Evaluating 25 parameter combinations across 3 folds  ■■■■■■■■■■■■                      36% |  ETA:  5s   ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         80% |  ETA:  1s cca_results # A tibble: 75 × 7    k_pred k_resp k_cca  fold  rmse cor_spatial cor_temporal     <int>  <int> <int> <int> <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl>  1      1      1     1     1 0.808       0.790        0.998  2      1      1     1     2 0.658       0.930        0.998  3      1      1     1     3 0.671       0.922        0.997  4      1      2     1     1 1.00        0.761        0.998  5      1      2     1     2 0.605       0.934        0.998  6      1      2     1     3 0.669       0.913        0.997  7      1      3     1     1 1.07        0.737        0.998  8      1      3     1     2 0.622       0.931        0.998  9      1      3     1     3 0.727       0.893        0.997 10      1      4     1     1 1.07        0.731        0.998 # ℹ 65 more rows cca_summary <- summarize_cv(cca_results, metric = "rmse") head(cca_summary) # A tibble: 6 × 10   k_pred k_resp k_cca rmse_mean rmse_sd cor_spatial_mean cor_spatial_sd    <int>  <int> <int>     <dbl>   <dbl>            <dbl>          <dbl> 1      5      5     5     0.270  0.0416            0.971         0.0199 2      5      4     4     0.287  0.0459            0.968         0.0212 3      4      4     4     0.288  0.0453            0.968         0.0210 4      5      3     3     0.313  0.0366            0.964         0.0208 5      4      3     3     0.314  0.0363            0.963         0.0207 6      3      3     3     0.316  0.0350            0.963         0.0207 # title: Cross-Validation and Tuning
text: prep_folds() creates balanced temporal folds cross-validation. assigns contiguous blocks time steps folds (random sampling), appropriate autocorrelated time series.
code: times <- st_get_dimension_values(fine, "time") folds <- prep_folds(times, kfolds = 5)  # Each fold is a vector of held-out times lengths(folds) [1] 8 7 7 7 7

title: Cross-Validation and Tuning
text: large grids many parameter combinations, tune_cca() supports parallel execution via furrr:
code: library(furrr) plan(multisession, workers = 4)  results <- tune_cca(cv_folds, k_pred = 1:10, k_resp = 1:10, parallel = TRUE)  plan(sequential) # clean up

title: Cross-Validation and Tuning
text: Start coarse, refine. Begin wide search (e.g., k_pred = 1:10) folds, narrow range folds. Use 3-5 folds. short time series (< 50 time steps), 3 folds preserves enough training data. longer series, 5 folds gives stable estimates. Rotation CV don’t mix. Varimax rotation makes modes non-orthogonal, subsetting pat[1:k] longer gives best rank-k approximation. CV functions require rotate = FALSE error rotation applied. Inspect multiple metrics. RMSE penalizes large errors; spatial correlation rewards pattern fidelity; temporal correlation rewards consistent bias. best k may differ across metrics – choose based application.

title: Statistical Downscaling with CCA
text: tidyeof supports statistical downscaling via Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA). workflow : Extract EOF patterns predictor (coarse) response (fine) fields Couple CCA find correlated modes co-variability Predict fine-scale fields new coarse-scale data vignette demonstrates full downscaling pipeline.

title: Statistical Downscaling with CCA
text: vignette, create synthetic downscaling problem. “fine” field bundled PRISM temperature data. “coarse” field derived spatially aggregating (simulating coarse-resolution model).
code: # Fine-resolution "observations" fine <- system.file("testdata/prism_test.RDS", package = "tidyeof") |>   readRDS()  # Coarse-resolution "model" -- aggregate to ~5x coarser grid # In practice, this would be a GCM or reanalysis product coarse <- fine |>   st_warp(cellsize = 0.2, method = "average", use_gdal = TRUE, no_data_value = -99999) |>   setNames(names(fine)) |>   st_set_dimensions("band",     values = st_get_dimension_values(fine, "time"),     names = "time") p1 <- ggplot() + geom_stars(data = fine[,,,1]) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(na.value = NA) + coord_sf() + theme_void() + ggtitle("Fine (PRISM)") p2 <- ggplot() + geom_stars(data = coarse[,,,1]) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(na.value = NA) + coord_sf() + theme_void() + ggtitle("Coarse (aggregated)") patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2)

title: Statistical Downscaling with CCA
text: Extract EOF patterns fields. number modes k controls much spatial detail retained. modes capture finer structure risk overfitting CCA step.
code: pred_pat <- patterns(coarse, k = 5) resp_pat <- patterns(fine, k = 5) plot(pred_pat, type = "eofs") + ggtitle("Predictor EOFs (coarse)") plot(resp_pat, type = "eofs") + ggtitle("Response EOFs (fine)")

title: Statistical Downscaling with CCA
text: couple() finds linear combinations predictor response PCs maximally correlated. k argument controls many CCA modes retain. Inspect canonical correlations – indicate strength predictor-response relationship mode:  Visualize canonical variates (optimally-correlated time series side):  Canonical spatial patterns show spatial structures predictor correspond structures response:  combined view shows everything together:
code: coupled <- couple(pred_pat, resp_pat, k = 3) coupled  ── Coupled Patterns Object ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── Method: cca CCA modes retained: 3 Canonical correlations: 1, 1, and 0.998 Predictor patterns: 5 PCs Response patterns: 5 PCs get_canonical_correlations(coupled) mode correlation correlation_squared 1    1   0.9999999           0.9999999 2    2   0.9999594           0.9999187 3    3   0.9981142           0.9962319 plot(coupled, type = "correlations") plot(coupled, type = "canonical", side = "both") plot(coupled, type = "canonical_patterns", side = "both") plot(coupled)

title: Statistical Downscaling with CCA
text: Inspect canonical correlations – indicate strength predictor-response relationship mode:  Visualize canonical variates (optimally-correlated time series side):  Canonical spatial patterns show spatial structures predictor correspond structures response:  combined view shows everything together:

title: Statistical Downscaling with CCA
text: predict() takes new coarse-resolution data produces fine-resolution prediction. Internally : projects new data onto predictor EOFs, applies CCA transform, multiplies response EOFs, adds response climatology.  don’t need full spatial reconstruction (e.g., diagnostics), set reconstruct = FALSE get predicted response amplitudes directly:
code: # Use the training data as a test (perfect predictor scenario) predicted <- predict(coupled, coarse) # Compare a single time step t_idx <- 12 p1 <- ggplot() + geom_stars(data = fine[,,,t_idx]) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(na.value = NA) + coord_sf() + theme_void() +   ggtitle("Observed (fine)") p2 <- ggplot() + geom_stars(data = predicted[,,,t_idx]) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(na.value = NA) + coord_sf() + theme_void() +   ggtitle("Predicted (downscaled)") patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2) pred_amps <- predict(coupled, coarse, reconstruct = FALSE) head(pred_amps) # A tibble: 6 × 6   time         PC1     PC2     PC3    PC4   PC5   <date>     <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> 1 2017-01-01 -758.  55.7   -36.1    34.8  -84.7 2 2017-02-01 -408.  12.9   -16.4    14.6  -45.7 3 2017-03-01 -143. -21.6     1.11    3.37 -15.5 4 2017-04-01 -137. -30.7    -0.637  -6.03 -15.9 5 2017-05-01  135. -17.5     5.78  -11.9   14.5 6 2017-06-01  421.  -0.942  12.3   -17.0   46.5

title: Statistical Downscaling with CCA
text: downscaling across multiple data sources (e.g., multiple reanalyses model runs), use common_patterns() extract shared EOF patterns. ensures sources projected EOF space, making amplitudes directly comparable. source gets patterns object source-specific amplitudes climatology, shared EOF spatial patterns: can couple one source’s patterns fine-scale observations, predict using another source’s patterns via predictor_patterns argument:
code: # Simulate two "sources" by splitting the time series times <- st_get_dimension_values(coarse, "time") source_a <- filter(coarse, time <= times[18]) source_b <- filter(coarse, time > times[18])  cpat <- common_patterns(list(a = source_a, b = source_b), k = 4, weight = TRUE) cpat  ── Common Patterns Object ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Sources: "a" and "b" Shared EOF modes: 4  ── Time steps per source ──  a: 18 b: 18  ── Processing Options ──  Scale: TRUE Rotate: FALSE Monthly: FALSE Weight: TRUE # The spatial patterns are identical; only amplitudes and climatology differ cpat$a$eofs stars object with 3 dimensions and 1 attribute attribute(s):               Min.     1st Qu.     Median       Mean    3rd Qu.      Max. weight  -0.2334799 -0.05031055 0.03787486 0.02307869 0.09097917 0.2537912 dimension(s):    from to offset delta refsys point      values x/y x     1 11   -118   0.2  NAD83 FALSE        NULL [x] y     1 11  43.98  -0.2  NAD83 FALSE        NULL [y] PC    1  4     NA    NA     NA    NA PC1,...,PC4 cpat$b$eofs stars object with 3 dimensions and 1 attribute attribute(s):               Min.     1st Qu.     Median       Mean    3rd Qu.      Max. weight  -0.2334799 -0.05031055 0.03787486 0.02307869 0.09097917 0.2537912 dimension(s):    from to offset delta refsys point      values x/y x     1 11   -118   0.2  NAD83 FALSE        NULL [x] y     1 11  43.98  -0.2  NAD83 FALSE        NULL [y] PC    1  4     NA    NA     NA    NA PC1,...,PC4 # Train CCA using source A coupled_a <- couple(cpat$a, resp_pat, k = 3)  # Predict using source B's data, with B's climatology predict(coupled_a, source_b_new, predictor_patterns = cpat$b)

title: EOF Analysis with tidyeof
text: tidyeof provides tools Empirical Orthogonal Function (EOF) analysis spatiotemporal data stored stars objects. EOF analysis decomposes spatiotemporal field : Spatial patterns (EOFs) – dominant modes variability Time series (principal components) – mode evolves time Eigenvalues – much variance mode explains vignette covers core EOF workflow: extracting patterns, interpreting results, reconstructing fields.

title: EOF Analysis with tidyeof
text: EOF analysis, helps understand climatological baseline. get_climatology() returns list mean sd stars objects.  Monthly climatology captures seasonal cycle:  Anomalies departure climatology. scale option additionally divides standard deviation, producing standardized anomalies. can restore original field anomalies climatology – exact inverses:
code: # Annual climatology clim <- get_climatology(temp) plot(clim$mean, main = "Mean temperature") monthly_clim <- get_climatology(temp, monthly = TRUE) plot(monthly_clim$mean) anom <- get_anomalies(temp) anom_scaled <- get_anomalies(temp, scale = TRUE) restored <- restore_climatology(anom, clim) max(abs(temp[[1]] - restored[[1]]), na.rm = TRUE) # ~machine epsilon 1.776357e-15 [°C]

title: EOF Analysis with tidyeof
text: main function patterns(). handles anomalization, optional area weighting, PCA, optional varimax rotation internally. Key arguments: k – number modes retain scale – standardize anomalies PCA (useful combining variables different units) rotate – apply varimax rotation localized, interpretable patterns monthly – use monthly climatology anomalization weight – area-weight grid cells (default TRUE; accounts latitude-dependent cell area)

title: EOF Analysis with tidyeof
text: Unrotated EOFs orthogonal, mathematical convenience always physically meaningful. Varimax rotation relaxes orthogonality constraint spatial patterns (keeping amplitudes uncorrelated), often producing localized interpretable modes.  Compare rotated vs unrotated patterns:
code: pat_rot <- patterns(temp, k = 4, rotate = TRUE) plot(pat_rot) p1 <- plot(pat, type = "eofs") + ggtitle("Unrotated") p2 <- plot(pat_rot, type = "eofs") + ggtitle("Rotated") patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2, ncol = 1)

code: recon <- reconstruct(pat) errors <- sapply(1:4, function(k) {   recon_k <- reconstruct(pat[1:k])   sqrt(mean((temp[[1]] - recon_k[[1]])^2, na.rm = TRUE)) })  data.frame(k = 1:4, rmse = round(errors, 4)) k   rmse 1 1 0.6414 2 2 0.2845 3 3 0.2469 4 4 0.2112 # pmax(.x, 0 * .x) preserves units recon |> mutate(across(everything(), ~pmax(.x, 0 * .x)))

title: Summary
text: tidyeof R package Empirical Orthogonal Function (EOF) analysis EOF-based statistical downscaling spatiotemporal data. provides complete pipeline raw climate fields downscaled predictions: extracting dominant modes variability via EOF decomposition, coupling predictor response fields Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA), validating results temporal cross-validation. package operates stars [@pebesma2023] spatiotemporal arrays integrates tidyverse ecosystem, making modern climate analysis methods accessible researchers across disciplines.

title: Summary
text: EOF analysis foundational tool climate science related fields decomposing spatiotemporal variability interpretable modes [@hannachi2007]. combined CCA, forms basis statistical downscaling — transferring information coarse-resolution climate models reanalyses finer spatial scales [@vonStorch1999; @barnett1987]. methods remain central climate impact assessment, paleoclimate reconstruction, regional climate analysis. Despite widespread use methods, existing R package provides integrated EOF--downscaling workflow built modern spatial data infrastructure. Python, eofs [@dawson2016] provides mature EOF solver NumPy, Iris, xarray interfaces, xeofs [@rieger2024] extends rotated EOF, Maximum Covariance Analysis, dask support. However, neither includes downscaling pipeline, neither serves R users. R, existing options fragmented. esd package [@benestad2015] offers comprehensive statistical downscaling predates stars sf spatial objects available CRAN. wql package provides basic eof() function oriented toward water quality analysis. Packages climate4R bundle [@iturbide2019] include EOF-based downscaling part large, monolithic framework. Researchers commonly implement EOF-CCA pipelines scratch using base R matrix operations, resulting duplicated effort inconsistent handling spatial metadata, area weighting, unit propagation. tidyeof addresses gap cohesive package handles full workflow — climatology computation anomalization EOF extraction, CCA coupling, prediction, cross-validation — preserving spatial coordinates, units, metadata throughout. building stars spatiotemporal arrays sf geometry, package integrates naturally R’s modern geospatial stack tidyverse grammar data manipulation.

title: Summary
text: package organized around three S3 classes mirror analytical workflow: patterns: EOF spatial patterns, amplitude time series, eigenvalues, climatology, computed via patterns() options area weighting, standardization, varimax rotation, monthly anomalization. Large datasets trigger automatic truncated SVD via irlba [@baglama2005] computational efficiency. coupled_patterns: CCA-coupled predictor response EOF patterns, computed via couple(). predict() method performs spatial downscaling projecting new coarse-resolution data learned CCA transformation produce fine-resolution fields. common_patterns: Joint EOF decomposition across multiple datasets via common_patterns(), ensuring consistent spatial patterns cross-source comparison — particularly useful coupling reanalyses paleoclimate reconstructions transferring models across climate products. class supports standard R generics (print, plot, summary, predict, [, $) EOF objects behave like familiar R objects. Pattern visualization uses ggplot2 scico perceptually uniform color scales. Diagnostics include scree plots North et al. [-@north1982] error bars mode separability, modified Rule N significance testing based Tracy-Widom distribution, teleconnection correlation maps FDR-corrected significance contours, canonical correlation visualization coupled patterns. Cross-validation functions (tune_eof, tune_cca) support data-driven selection EOF truncation CCA dimensionality using contiguous temporal folds, pre-computed patterns enabling efficient grid search hyperparameter combinations. avoids common pitfall selecting modes explained variance alone without assessing predictive skill [@wilks2011].

title: Summary
text: earlier version package used @gauthier2022 downscale snowpack variability across western United States, coupling large-scale atmospheric circulation patterns station-level snow water equivalent observations via CCA. current version supports several active research programs spanning climate science archaeology. provides downscaling infrastructure paleoclimate reconstruction studies medieval plague dynamics, coupling PHYDA paleo-reanalysis [@steiger2018] high-resolution CHELSA climatologies drive epidemiological demographic models scales relevant disease transmission. also used temperature precipitation downscaling US Southwest archaeological demographic simulation. case, package’s cross-validation framework enables rigorous assessment downscaling skill using predictions downstream models.
